# Figure description: Figure 3.jpg

**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type
Local geology map (bedrock and surficial geology) with mineral occurrence overlay, presented at 1:100,000 scale. Includes an inset location map showing the regional context within Western Australia.

---

## Coverage and Subject
The map covers the area around **Exploration Licence E20-742** in the **Coe district, Murchison region, Western Australia**, centred approximately on MGA94 Zone 50 coordinates **580,000–590,000 E** and **6,975,000–6,990,000 N**. The map sheet context corresponds to the **COE** 1:100,000 sheet. Named localities visible include: Dardanubba, Barkly Downs Outcamp, Harris Heights (437 m), City of Chester, Fleece P, Emily W, Maralinga Downs B, Railway B, Extension P, Wallace P, No. 2B, No. 3W, No. 4W, Roddy W, Milly B, Milly Stn, 9 Mile W, Daighton W, Wattagee Hill, and various pastoral station references. The inset map identifies the location relative to Port Hedland, Geraldton, Kalgoorlie, Perth, and Esperance.

---

## Key Content

**Tenure:**
The thick black polygon delineating **E20-742** occupies a roughly irregular rectangular area in the north-central portion of the map, encompassing approximately 6,979,000–6,989,000 N and 578,000–592,000 E. The tenement boundary has a stepped, irregular shape suggesting it has been shaped around existing granted tenements.

**Geology (colours and units):**
The geology is rendered in standard Western Australian Geological Survey colours. Key units visible include:

- **Pale yellow / cream (Qw, Qg, Qc):** Quaternary surficial sediments — windblown sand, alluvium, and colluvium — covering much of the central and western parts of the map. These are labelled Qw (wind-blown sand), Qg (gilgai/alluvial), and Qc (calcrete/colluvium).
- **Medium yellow (Auz, Auz2, Afo, Afv, Abb, Auzk):** Archean felsic volcanic and volcaniclastic units — likely rhyolites and felsic tuffs of the **Coe Domain, Yilgarn Craton**. These dominate the central and southern portions of the map inside and around the tenement.
- **Green (teal/dark green irregular elongate bodies):** Archean mafic–ultramafic units — likely komatiites and basalts. Several elongate bodies are visible, notably in the northeast (Wattagee Hill area), the west, and several small pods in the south. These represent greenstone belt remnants.
- **Blue-grey (narrow linear body trending NNW through centre):** Likely an intrusive dyke or narrow sedimentary unit cutting through the Archean basement.
- **Purple/mauve (south-west):** A distinct unit in the A Hill area (462 m elevation noted), possibly a differentiated intrusive or banded iron formation (BIF)-associated unit.
- **Red-pink (south-east):** A prominent unit near "Hidden Treasure" area — possibly a granitoid intrusion or iron-rich lithology (labelled partially as "As").
- **Light green/olive (Abu, Abg):** Archean mafic/intermediate units, visible near Dardanubba and Harris Heights areas, inside the northern part of the tenement.

**Structural features:**
Linear contacts and unit elongations suggest a dominant **NNW–SSE structural grain** consistent with Murchison greenstone belt architecture. The narrow blue-grey dyke/unit trending through the centre reinforces this fabric.

**Mineral occurrences (MINEDEX symbols):**
Numerous historical mineral occurrences are plotted across the map:
- **Yellow squares (Precious Metal — gold):** The most abundant symbol; densely clustered in the south (Cuddingwarra/City of Chester/Extension P area) and scattered throughout the tenement and surrounds. Multiple occurrences are within E20-742.
- **Red triangles (Base Metal):** Several occurrences, notably near Dardanubba (northern tenement boundary) and in the Wattagee Hill area.
- **Blue triangles (Construction Material):** One occurrence near City of Chester area.
- **Black triangles (Energy):** One occurrence in the north near Dardanubba.
- **Blue square (Iron):** One occurrence in the south-central area (near "Glory United" label).
- **Red/orange crosses (Other — MINEDEX non-minerals):** Several scattered across the south (Maralinga Downs B area and City of Chester).
- **Yellow squares with outlines (Precious Metal):** Dominant occurrence type — well over 20 occurrences on and around the tenement.

**Elevation:** Harris Heights noted at **437 m**, A Hill at **462 m**.
